what_it_does: Acceptance test for a generalized 'marks' store built on FsStore: it writes marks with claim/span targets and mark_type payloads, retrieves them by target and by mark_type, checks persistence across reload, ensures pre-existing findings remain byte-for-byte unchanged, and enforces fail-loud structural validation.
observations: Uses a temporary directory as the store root and instantiates FsStore directly.
Defines a simple global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and prints.
Tests marks_for(target), marks_by_type(mark_type), and all_marks() against claim:// and span:// style opaque target strings.
Verifies append-only semantics: re-running a mark-pass appends a second record for the same target.
Verifies cross-target aggregation via marks_by_type while marks_for stays single-target.
Reloads a second FsStore over the same root to assert persistence.
Writes an old-style finding first and re-checks that findings_for/all_findings are unchanged after marks are added.
Asserts marks never leak into findings and findings never leak into marks.
Uses a refused(label, fn) helper to assert ValueError on missing/empty target or mark_type.
No cleanup of the temporary directory is shown.
standouts: Strong preservation guarantee: old findings.jsonl corpus must remain byte-for-byte untouched by marks.
Marks generalize the finding record along two axes: arbitrary target (claim/span/address) and mark_type retrieval.
Fail-loud structural validation: missing or empty target/mark_type is refused, and no partial write remains.
conventions: Inline narrative docstring explains design intent and separation of lanes.
Simple imperative test script style that runs on import.
Uses illustrative opaque URI strings without parsing them in this file.
concerns: The test depends on FsStore methods whose implementation is not shown in this file.
Global PASS counter and side-effect prints make it less suitable as a reusable pytest module.
No teardown or temp-directory cleanup is visible.
Illustrative target/mark_type strings are not validated here, so the test cannot catch grammar errors.
notes: A file-local acceptance script that proves the marks store generalizes findings while keeping the legacy finding corpus isolated and unchanged.
questions: What is the exact FsStore implementation and how does it physically separate marks from findings?
Where is the claim/span/address grammar validated (C1 contract / Suite S0 gate)?
Where is the mark-types registry defined and how are mark_type ids assigned?
Is this script intended to run under pytest or as a standalone script?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the executed checks: it validates marks store primitives, dual retrieval, persistence, preservation, and structural refusal.
apparent_themes: Store generalization without breaking legacy data
Fail-loud validation at the storage boundary
Separation of concerns between store, grammar, and registry
intention_signals: Establish marks as a first-class, generalized extension of the finding record that adds target and mark_type retrieval axes, while strictly isolating the pre-existing finding corpus so downstream consumers such as burn_down counts remain unaffected.
novelty: Generalizes the finding store into a marks store using a sibling-leaf layout that preserves old findings byte-for-byte and adds cross-target aggregation by mark_type.
contribution: An acceptance-level specification and verification that the marks store primitives support claim/span targets, retrieval by target and mark_type, persistence, append-only behavior, legacy-finding preservation, and structural fail-loud validation.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for FsStore marks primitives verifying claim/span targets, mark_type retrieval, persistence, legacy-finding preservation, and structural refusal.
intention_for_embedding: Validate that the marks store generalizes the finding store without polluting the existing finding corpus.

symbols.does: Asserts a condition, increments a global pass counter, and prints an ok/fail message.
symbols.description: A lightweight test helper used throughout the script. It takes a label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL label on failure, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints a status line.

symbols.does: Asserts that a callable raises ValueError, treating a raised exception as a passing check.
symbols.description: A helper that wraps a zero-argument function and checks it raises ValueError. It is used to verify that malformed mark records—missing or empty target or mark_type—are rejected fail-loudly by the store.
